macro_segments.title: Introduction and Initial Case: Hiring Through an Agent

micro_segments.title: Marketplace workers and conditional payment arrangements

Defense of Rav's position and resolution of contradictions
Extended cases: weekly, monthly, and annual wages

macro_segments.title: Multiple Prohibitions Violated by Wage Withholding

micro_segments.title: Five names (Chamesh Shemot) violated in wage withholding
Difference between cheating (Oshek) and stealing (Gezel) according to Rav Chisda
Disputes on Oshek and Gezel definitions: Rav Sheshet vs Abaye
Rava's unified position on Oshek and Gezel as same violation

macro_segments.title: Extension to Animals, Tools, and Conditional Payment

micro_segments.title: Mishna extending obligations to animal and tool rentals
Conditional exemption when claim is made and credit arrangements
Worker's oath and time limitations for claiming wages

macro_segments.title: Status of Ger Toshav and Attribution of Mishna

micro_segments.title: Application of obligations to Ger Toshav (resident alien)
Determination of Mishna attribution: not Tanna Kamma or Rabbi Yosei

macro_segments.title: Tannaitic Dispute: Three Positions on Ger Toshav and Behemah/Kelim

micro_segments.title: Tanna Kamma's position excluding Ger Toshav via Gezeira Shava Sachir Sachir

macro_segments.title: Justification for Excluding Ger Toshav and Including Animals/Tools

micro_segments.title: Distinction based on 'Ki Ani Hu' (because he is poor) for person-based obligations
Rabbi Chananya's teaching on poverty-based distinction
Tanna Kamma's use of Ki Ani Hu for prioritizing poor workers
Extended discussion on embarrassment and necessity for rich vs poor workers

macro_segments.title: Reconciling Tanna de-Vei Rabbi Yishmael's Distinctions

micro_segments.title: Question on apparent contradictions in Tanna de-Vei Rabbi Yishmael's position
Resolution through 'Peulato Itcha' (work done with you) limiting Bal Talin
Exclusion of Ger Toshav through Recha (your friend) language in pasuk
Inclusion of animals and tools within Mamon Recha concept

macro_segments.title: Tanna Kamma's Additional Drashas and the Amaleki Exclusion

micro_segments.title: Question on Tanna Kamma's use of Recha for additional distinctions

Meiri's principle on ethical societies and wage protections

macro_segments.title: Rabbi Yosei bar Rabbi Yehuda's Source for Exclusions

micro_segments.title: Rabbi Yosei's use of Lo Talin Peulat Sachir for distinctions
Rav Assi's principle on minimal work and Bal Talin application
Conclusion: any work one puts oneself into triggers obligations
